content.problemStatement:
Chef is watching a football match. The current score is A:BA:BA:B, that is, team 111 has scored AAA goals and team 222 has scored BBB goals. Chef wonders if it is possible for the score to become C:DC:DC:D at a later point in the game (i.e. team 111 has scored CCC goals and team 222 has scored DDD goals). Can you help Chef by answering his question?


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains two integers AAA and BBB - the intial number of goals team 111 and team 222 have scored respectively.
The second line of each test case contains two integers CCC and DDD - the final number of goals team 111 and team 222 must be able to score respectively.


Output Format
For each testcase, output `POSSIBLE` if it is possible for the score to become C:DC:DC:D at a later point in the game, `IMPOSSIBLE` otherwise.


You may print each character of `POSSIBLE` and `IMPOSSIBLE` in uppercase or lowercase (for example, `possible`, `pOSsiBLe`, `Possible` will be considered identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains two integers A and B — the current goals of team 1 and team 2. The second line contains two integers C and D — the desired future goals of team 1 and team 2.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "POSSIBLE" if the score (C:D) can be reached from (A:B) by only increasing scores, otherwise output "IMPOSSIBLE".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: 3 >= 1 and 5 >= 5, so POSSIBLE.\nTest case 2: 2 < 3 (C < A), so IMPOSSIBLE.\nTest case 3: Scores are already equal, so POSSIBLE.

solution.approach: The scores can only increase as the match progresses. Therefore the final score (C, D) is reachable from the current score (A, B) iff C >= A and D >= B. For each test case we simply check these two inequalities and print the corresponding answer.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case is processed in constant time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
